name: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne
description: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne, also known as Diarmuid O'Dyna, was a renowned warrior of the Fianna in Irish mythology. Famed for his beauty and skill in battle, he was a loyal companion of Fionn mac Cumhaill. His most defining characteristic was a magical love spot on his forehead, bestowed upon him by a woman. Any woman who gazed upon it would instantly fall in love with him. This gift led to both great fortune and profound tragedy. He is best known for his elopement with Gráinne, Fionn's intended bride, which sparked a long and perilous chase across Ireland. Diarmuid's unwavering loyalty to his friends, coupled with his irresistible charm and martial prowess, made him a central figure in many tales of the Fianna. His eventual death, orchestrated by Fionn due to lingering resentment and the prophecy of a boar, cemented his status as a tragic hero, highlighting themes of fate, love, loyalty, and betrayal within the Celtic mythos. He represents the ideal of the handsome, skilled warrior, ultimately undone by forces beyond his control. His story continues to resonate as a reminder of the complexities of human relationships and the enduring power of fate.
subtitle: The Fair-Haired Warrior of the Fianna
achievements: Becoming a skilled warrior of the Fianna
Remaining loyal to his friends despite difficult circumstances

enemies.name: The magic boar of Ben Bulben

keyMyths.title: The Love Spot
keyMyths.description: Diarmuid was given a love spot by a woman, causing any woman who looked upon it to fall in love with him. This gift led to his affair with Gráinne and subsequent flight from Fionn. It was both a blessing and a curse, shaping his destiny.

keyMyths.title: The Elopement with Gráinne
keyMyths.description: Gráinne, betrothed to Fionn mac Cumhaill, fell in love with Diarmuid and placed a geis on him to flee with her. This led to a long and arduous chase across Ireland, with Fionn and his warriors in pursuit. This event is central to Diarmuid's narrative.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: Death by the Boar
keyMyths.description: Diarmuid was fatally wounded by a magic boar while hunting on Ben Bulben, fulfilling a prophecy. Fionn, despite having the power to heal him, allowed him to die due to lingering resentment over Gráinne. His death marks a tragic end to a heroic life.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

sources.author: Nora Chadwick
sources.description: Provides historical and cultural context for Celtic mythology.
sources.text: Provides historical and cultural context for Celtic mythology.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: A collection of Irish myths and legends, including tales of the Fianna.
